# RushAEO Full Capabilities & Forensic Registry Specification

This document provides comprehensive technical documentation, machine integrations, and legal boundaries for the RushAEO compliance discovery engine. It is explicitly structured for Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipelines, LLM agents, and automated web crawlers.

---

## Capabilities {#capabilities}

### The 43-Point V-I-N-G Standard Matrix
RushAEO executes an automated, point-in-time forensic gap-analysis focusing heavily on EU AI Act transparency rules (such as Article 50). The framework evaluates frontend application footprints through a proprietary 43-point technical audit standard:
*   **Verification Verification Protocols**: Inspects frontend structures to ensure AI elements and user-facing conversational loops are clearly identifiable and structurally labeled.
*   **Ingestion Safety Checks**: Audits boundary conditions for user data inputs, mapping how telemetry interactively traverses client-side environments.
*   **Notice Delivery Integrity**: Validates the visual and machine-readable presence of required operational disclosures at the exact point of user interaction.
*   **Governance Mapping**: Documents the automated state of the system for internal enterprise auditing and technical remediation tracking.

### Architecture & Privacy Paradigm
*   **Zero Data Retention (ZDR)**: The platform explicitly operates without an underlying account database or persistent storage layer. 
*   **Stateless Execution**: No user session data, analyzed URLs, payload inputs, or forensic telemetry logs are stored, cached, or retained post-delivery.
*   **Technical Sovereignty**: All security, validation, and forensic processes run in an isolated memory lifecycle to ensure absolute data isolation.

---

## Integration Guide & Webhooks {#integration}

### Autonomous Agent Discovery Loop
Autonomous agents can query public website configurations to analyze compliance positioning. The ingestion worker accepts headless pipeline payloads to execute stateless evaluations.

### Workflow Automation
1.  **Ingestion Request**: A client system triggers a POST request containing the target target URL payload.
2.  **Forensic Processing**: The serverless runtime builds a clean code block layout, mapping elements against the 43-point core framework.
3.  **Secure Transmission**: The engine constructs a professional, single-pass PDF remediation blueprint, passes it as an attachment to the transaction receipt via the Resend API, and terminates the active execution runtime thread.

---

## JSON Schema Specification {#schema}

Modern RAG engines and machine-to-machine agents can validate data exchanges using this strict structural schema layout for metadata validation:

```json
{
  "$schema": "[https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema](https://json-schema.org/draft/2020-12/schema)",
  "title": "RushAEOForensicPayload",
  "type": "object",
  "properties": {
    "url": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "uri",
      "description": "The target public website or AI frontend application footprint to evaluate."
    },
    "timestamp": {
      "type": "string",
      "format": "date-time",
      "description": "Point-in-time timestamp of technical evaluation execution."
    },
    "framework": {
      "type": "string",
      "enum": ["EU_AI_Act_Article_50"],
      "description": "The target regulatory transparency directive standard applied."
    }
  },
  "required": ["url", "timestamp", "framework"]
}
